exemplar.title: To what extent do the different architectures of MongoDB and MySQL when used with Python affect their performance with large amounts of data insertions?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the different architectures of MongoDB and MySQL when used with Python affect their performance with large amounts of data insertions?

strengths: Title and introduction clearly communicate focus on database architecture performance
Research question is precisely stated and maintained throughout the investigation
Rationale for using Python libraries is well explained and linked to experimental needs
weaknesses: Some verbosity and occasional repetition dilute the effectiveness of communication
Reliance on ChatGPT-generated code without validation reduces methodological rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently communicates purpose and focus, with a tightly connected research question. Methodological description is mostly complete, though some selection choices (ChatGPT code, lack of replication) weaken the highest-band rigor. This performance fits the lower end of the top band, meriting 5/6.

strengths: Clear, concise explanation of SQL vs NoSQL concepts with appropriate citations
Generally accurate and consistent use of technical terminology
Solid application of secondary sources to support background understanding
weaknesses: Minor factual inaccuracies about scalability limits
Occasional misnaming of libraries (e.g. âPyMongoDBâ) reduces precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good subject knowledge and mostly accurate use of terminology. A few superficial statements and naming errors prevent it reaching the top band. The performance aligns with the upper end of the 3â4 band, justifying 4/6.

strengths: Primary data and selected literature consistently address the research question
Analysis is data-driven, with clear identification of performance inflection points
Discussion connects results to architectural theory and acknowledges key limitations
weaknesses: No replication trials, statistical tests or error analysis limit robustness
Background section is largely descriptive rather than critically tied back to the question
Alternative interpretations (e.g. I/O bottlenecks) are not fully explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research application, analysis and evaluation all fall solidly in the 7â9 band. The student shows good critical thinking, though missing statistical rigor and deeper evaluation preclude the highest marks. An 8/12 reflects strong performance with room for deeper critique.

strengths: Structure is clear and logical, with a helpful table of contents and coherent sequencing
Diagrams of SQL and NoSQL models are well integrated and aid reader understanding
weaknesses: Several figures are low-resolution and some captions are mismatched or lack descriptive titles
Layout inconsistencies (spacing, citation formatting) occasionally hinder readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs structure fully supports understanding, warranting the top band for that strand. Layout issues, however, place it in the middle band. Combining these, the Presentation criterion merits 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student describes hardware, software, control factors and provides pseudo-code, showing a mostly complete method.  Relevant primary data are gathered through timed experiments and a reasonable range of secondary sources is cited for background.  However, reliance on ChatGPT-generated code, absence of replication trials, and limited consideration of alternative configurations signal only partial evidence of informed source/method selection.  These limitations keep the work within the middle band, at its upper edge.

The student plots multiple graphs, calculates insert-per-second metrics and identifies inflection points (â30 M rows).  Arguments are largely data-driven and conclusions are generally supported.  Nevertheless, statistical testing, error analysis and replication are missing, limiting depth.  The analysis is therefore âgood and relevant with mostly supported conclusionsâ, meriting a solid 7 within the 7-9 range.

The essay follows the prescribed structure (abstract omitted but not required for CS EE), includes table of contents, headings, consistent sequencing of methodology-results-analysis-evaluation-conclusion, and appendices.  The arrangement clearly aids reader understanding, so the upper mark of the top presentation band is justified.

The discussion links observed trends to architectural theory, acknowledges limitations (hardware, simplified schema, default configs) and suggests implications for real-world deployment.  Argumentation is coherent, yet evaluation is occasionally surface-level and alternative interpretations (e.g. library overhead, I/O bottlenecks) are not fully explored.  This reflects a competent, if not outstanding, critical evaluationâthus the lower end of the 7-9 band.

Technical termsâe.g. bulk inserts, primary key, schema, horizontal scalingâare used frequently and mostly accurately.  Minor slips ("insert many()" instead of insert_many, "PyMongoDB" mis-naming) and occasional grammatical noise reduce precision.  Consistency and correctness are nonetheless adequate for a secure placement in the 3-4 band at its top end.

Primary data collected (20 insertion sizes across two systems) are consistently linked back to the focused question, and secondary literature is used to justify expectations.  Data are relevant and sufficiently detailed to support comparison.  Some methodological weaknesses noted earlier prevent classification as âexcellentâ, but the clear, repeated alignment of research with the question reaches the lower boundary of the 7-9 band.

Figures, tables and code blocks are present, but some captions are repetitive or mismatched, and several images are low-resolution.  Line spacing/formatting irregularities (fragmented code, breaks) occasionally hinder readability.  Overall layout considerations are only partially successful, aligning with the mid bandâs description.

The student demonstrates solid understanding of relational vs non-relational concepts, insertion batching, and scalability, employing appropriate secondary literature.  Explanations of SQL tables, JSON documents and indexing reflect generally correct knowledge, though some statements are superficial and a few inaccuracies ("MySQL is not designed for scalability past 30 M rows") appear.  Overall, knowledge is good but not exemplary, earning the upper score of the 3-4 band.

The research question appears verbatim in both the title and introduction and is sharply delimited (effect of differing architectures on bulk insert speed in Python).  Subsequent sections, methodology, results and discussion all reference this same performance metric, showing effective connection.  While the question might have been refined further to isolate variables (e.g. single-threaded bulk inserts, default configs), it meets the descriptor of clear, focused and effectively connected, hence a strong top-band but not perfect score.

The student states the focus explicitly in the title and reiterates it in the introduction.  The purposeâto compare insertion-performance of two specific database architectures in Pythonâis maintained throughout, and headings, graphs and data tables align directly with this focus.  Minor digressions into broad background do not seriously detract, so the work fulfils descriptors for a clear and appropriate purpose, but some verbosity and occasional repetition prevent a flawless communication, justifying a score toward the lower end of the top band.

annotations.comment: The explanation of NoSQL storage techniques is clear and concise, effectively contrasting with SQL.
Shorten figure captions and reference long URLs in the bibliography to improve read-through and layout coherence.
Standardize all in-text citations (year, punctuation, italics) to ensure a uniform scholarly presentation across sections.
The rationale for choosing Python and its libraries is well explained, linking tool selection to experiment requirements.
The methodology clearly details hardware, software and variables, demonstrating informed selection of experimental tools.
Reliance on ChatGPT-generated code is noted but its reproducibility and validation are not addressed, weakening methodological rigor.
Include a plan for replication trials, error analysis and software version control to strengthen the robustness of the method.
The title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on database architectures and their performance implications.
The research question is stated prominently and remains tightly connected to subsequent sections, demonstrating strong focus.
The word count is provided and under the 4,000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
The detailed table of contents aids navigation, showing clear alignment between sections and page numbers.
Several TOC entries lack page numbers for later sections and appendices; ensure all headings reference correct pages.
The introduction succinctly establishes context and states the research question, setting a clear purpose.
Some introductory sentences are verbose and repetitive; consider tightening language to maintain reader engagement and clarity.
Introduce at least one citation in the introduction to support the claim about database importance, enhancing the evidence base.
The background section provides clear definitions of SQL and NoSQL databases with relevant citations.
The background is largely descriptive without linking concepts back to the research question; integrate critical comparisons to strengthen relevance.
Streamline background definitions by referring to terminology sections later to avoid redundancy and maintain focus.
The SQL architecture diagram is informative and well integrated, aiding visual understanding of table structures.
Caption text is overly long and disrupts layout; relocate full URL to references and use concise descriptive captions.
An OCR glitch shows â<math display=inline>2011</math>â in the text; correct this to standard citation format.
Correct the inline citation formatting by removing math tags to ensure consistent presentation of references.
Figure 2 effectively illustrates the NoSQL document model, supporting understanding of key database types.
The caption âFigure 2â lacks a descriptive title, reducing clarity; include a brief explanatory phrase.
Revise figure captions to include descriptive labels (e.g., âNoSQL Document Storage Modelâ) for better standalone comprehension.
The section on MongoDB and MySQL provides balanced pros and cons, demonstrating good critical engagement with sources.
Citations in this section vary in formatting style (access dates, punctuation); apply a consistent referencing style.
The text misnames âPyMySQLâ as âPythonMySQLâ and âPyMongoDBâ instead of âPyMongoâ; correct library names for accuracy.
Ensure terminology for external libraries matches official documentation to maintain technical precision.
Code snippets lack consistent indentation and error-handling details, which may hinder reproducibility.
Annotate code with precise version numbers and dependencies in comments to facilitate replication and review by peers.

rubricData.name: Computer Science EE
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: The Computer Science Extended Essay is a 4,000-word independent research project that allows students to explore a specific topic within computer science. This essay emphasizes technical analysis and original thought, encouraging students to delve into areas such as algorithm efficiency, software development, or the impact of computing technologies. By engaging in the EE, students apply their theoretical knowledge to practical scenarios, enhancing their problem-solving and analytical skills, while contributing original insights to the field of computer science.
